title: Wire sensors with timeouts and reschedule mode
description: Use sensors to wait for external readiness without exhausting worker slots, with explicit timeouts and failure handling.
body: Use when a pipeline must wait for an external file, partition, or upstream job. Do not poll in a tight loop that holds a worker slot for hours — sensors can starve the pool. Actions: use reschedule/deferrable sensor mode so waiting does not occupy a slot, set a timeout, and define what happens on timeout (fail and alert, not silently skip). Validation signals include a sensor releasing its slot while waiting and a timeout that fails loudly with context. Risk boundary: a slot-holding sensor can deadlock the scheduler under many concurrent waits; if sensors block the pool, escalate to deferrable mode before scaling workers to mask it.
